Houston Rhinoplasty Course Houston Methodist Dept. of Otolaryngology / Head & Neck Surgery

2026 Houston Annual
Rhinoplasty Course

Presented by the Houston Methodist Department of Otolaryngology
Division of Facial Plastic & Reconstructive Surgery

Date
March 21, 2026
Location
Houston Methodist Academic Institute
Format
Lecture + Lab
About the Course

Advancing the Next Generation
of Rhinoplasty Surgeons

The Houston Rhinoplasty Course is a focused educational event designed for residents across the Houston metropolitan area. This one-day intensive program brings together leading faculty in facial plastic and reconstructive surgery for a full day of expert lectures, case-based discussion, and hands-on cadaver dissection.

The course bridges didactic instruction with practical application — offering residents direct exposure to advanced techniques in a collaborative, peer-learning environment.

Course Objectives
01

Understand the principles of nasal anatomy, patient assessment for nasal obstruction, and fundamental concepts for both functional and cosmetic rhinoplasty.

02

Explore new techniques and innovations in rhinoplasty currently advancing the field of facial plastic surgery.

03

Collaborate with residents across programs in case-based discussion led by expert faculty.

04

Participate in hands-on cadaver dissections to apply the concepts learned during the morning didactic sessions.

A Message from the Course Director
Welcome to the Houston Methodist Rhinoplasty Course; an exciting and collaborative educational experience that reflects our deep commitment to advancing resident training and surgical excellence, building on a strong foundation of multidisciplinary learning and innovation.

Dr. Coviello and I are grateful to our outstanding faculty, generous sponsors, and engaged participants for making this course possible, and extend a special thank you to Dr. Takashima for his continued support—your collective contributions are what truly bring this vision to life.
Anthony E. Brissett, MD
Course Director · Houston Methodist ENT Specialists

Faculty & Speakers

Keynote Speaker
Jose Barrera, MD will deliver the keynote address and lead the afternoon cadaver dissection lab, covering core principles and advanced techniques in preservation rhinoplasty.
